items.title: Dow jumps nearly 600 points to record close; Nasdaq slides as chip stocks suffer
items.content: Dow rose 594.83 points (+1.14%) to 52,900.07 record close. S&P 500 flat at 7,483.24. Nasdaq dropped 0.8% to 25,832.67. Tech sell-off driven by chip stocks: Micron -7%, AMD -4.3%, Marvell -9.8%, SanDisk -14%. Tesla fell 7.5% despite strong deliveries. Apple gained 4.8%. Softer-than-expected June jobs report pushed back expectations of imminent Fed rate hike.

items.title: Dow closes at all-time high ahead of Independence Day holiday
items.content: Dow notched second straight record close. S&P 500 flat, Nasdaq 100 fell 0.8%. Chip stocks fell for second day as investors questioned AI valuation sustainability. Stocks edged higher ahead of July 4 holiday weekend as investors reacted to key economic reports.

items.title: Dow Jumps 600 Points to Record, While Tech Stock Sell-Off Sends Nasdaq Lower
items.content: Dow recorded second straight record close with ~600 point gain. Tech sell-off continued as S&P 500 ended flat. Major indexes posted gains for the week.

items.title: US equity indices closed mixed on Thursday; Dow record, Nasdaq 100 fell 0.8%
items.content: Dow climbed 595 points to new record high amid strength in traditional sectors as softer-than-expected June jobs report pushed back expectations of imminent Fed hike. Apple +4.8%, Visa and Walmart +3%. Meta -4.9% said may monetize excess compute. Micron -7%, Applied Materials -7.4%, SanDisk -14%, Marvell -9.8%. S&P 500 at 7,496 on July 3 (+0.17%). US500 up 20.33% YoY.

items.title: June 2026 jobs report: US economy added 57,000 jobs, softer than expected
items.content: US added just 57,000 jobs in June, less than half the 115,000 economists forecast. Unemployment rate ticked down to 4.2% from 4.3%. June was the lightest hiring month since February. Seema Shah (Principal Asset Management): 'paints a softer picture of the labor market than investors have become accustomed to, but should ultimately be welcomed by markets.'

items.title: Steady but not strong: US job growth slowed in June
items.content: Employment gains softer in June, unemployment rate dropped as fewer people were in labor market. 57,000 jobs added vs 115,000 expected.

items.title: Hang Seng Index rose 0.8% to close at 23,055 on Thursday
items.content: HSI +174 points (+0.76%) to 23,055 on Thursday. Gains in technology, financial and retail stocks. Bargain hunting after recent losses despite tech-driven selloff on Wall Street. Tencent +0.1%, Xiaomi +4.2%, Meituan +3.1%, Innovent Biologics +7.0%, AIA +1.4%. Kevin Warsh said inflation expectations eased. HK50 rose to 23,366 on July 3 (+1.35%). HSI down 7.47% over past month.

items.title: OpenAI in early talks to give 5% stake to US government
items.content: OpenAI reportedly in early-stage talks to give 5% stake in ChatGPT developer to US government as AI startup seeks to defuse mounting political pressure in Washington. Reported by Financial Times, citing sources familiar with the talks.

items.title: PCE inflation hits 4.1% in May, highest since April 2023
items.content: Core PCE 3.4% annual rate, highest since October 2023. Headline PCE 4.1% annual rate. Monthly PCE accelerated 0.4%. Energy prices largest source of gains (+4% monthly). Housing costs +0.3%. Financial services and insurance +1.2%. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh reaffirmed commitment to bring inflation down. Traders expect Fed rate hike in September.

items.title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis: Iran closed strait again on June 20
items.content: On June 19 Trump announced renewed ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Iran closed strait again on June 20 citing Israeli actions as violation. On June 27 US Navy JMIC announced widened route through Strait of Hormuz. Ceasefire and nuclear talks ongoing.

items.title: Earnings calendar for week ahead (July 6-10)
items.content: Q2 earnings season begins in earnest during second full week of July. PepsiCo among early reporters. II.co.uk notes: after strong start to 2026, Iran conflict likely impacted many US companies during March-June Q2 period.

items.title: Fed rate hike expectations pushed back after soft June jobs report
items.content: Softer-than-expected June jobs report pushed back expectations of imminent Fed rate hike. PCE at 4.1% but some Fed officials noted inflation expectations easing. Market pricing for September hike lowered slightly.
